content.text:
{
  "dimension": "intervention-quality",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach's moves are tightly matched to live reads and sequenced with care. Turn 2 catches the laugh as a tell rather than engaging the victim frame. Turn 4 reads the 'expertise' as protection — a precise read-to-move match. Turn 6 pivots from the story of unavailable partners to the inverse question (what happens when someone shows up), refusing to collude with the disdain or give partner-picking advice. Turn 8 is the key AoA move — 'what happens in your body when someone pursues *you*' — exactly the breakthrough-condition question, asked at the moment she has just performed the 'I want someone who shows up' line. Turn 10 holds her at the edge ('you laughed — but stay there') rather than letting her deflect, and Turn 12 uses minimal encouragement ('say more') to let the felt material continue surfacing. Turn 14 asks what she was defending against — landing precisely the 'what are you defending' question from expected territory. The sequence honors feeling before story and want-under-should, and the coach holds silence/space rather than rushing in with frameworks or attachment-style diagnoses.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the laugh as a defense signal; refuses to engage the victim story directly."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the expertise/mapping as possible protection — precise read-to-move match on defensiveness-as-identity."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Inverts the frame from 'unavailable partners' to 'someone showing up' — refuses partner-picking advice."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "The pivotal move: directs attention to the body when she is pursued. Exactly the breakthrough question, timed after she performed the want."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Holds her at the deflection edge ('you laughed — but stay there'); does not let the laugh dissolve the moment."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Minimal 'say more' move keeps her in the felt territory rather than steering."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Asks what she's defending against — lands the 'what are you defending' question; opens the door to the abyss without naming it prematurely."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["defensiveness-as-identity-signal", "what-are-you-defending", "feeling-the-unfelt-emotion"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["deconstruct-the-victim-to-release-anger", "i-dont-buy-your-story", "if-i-were-inherently-good"],
  "notes": "Conversation cuts off just before the abyss/receiving-state could fully land. Coach has set it up well; the next move (holding silence, or 'what would it be like if he were inherently good') would be the test. As of turn 14, moves and timing are on the golden path."
}
